title: Cambodia Coffee Beans Online: How to Order OCC Fine Robusta Anywhere in the World
Author: OCC Team
Summary: Buy Cambodia coffee beans online from Origin Coffee Cambodia. Fine Robusta single-origin beans shipped to the US, EU, Taiwan, Japan, and Australia. Order guide, pricing, and shipping times.
Excerpt: Ordering authentic specialty coffee from Cambodia has never been easier. Origin Coffee Cambodia ships globally to most international destinations within 7-12 days.
Keywords: Cambodia coffee beans online, buy Cambodia coffee online, order Cambodia coffee, OCC beans online, Cambodia coffee worldwide shipping
Content:
## Cambodia Coffee Beans Online: How to Order OCC Fine Robusta Anywhere in the World

Three years ago, ordering authentic specialty coffee from Cambodia meant knowing someone who knew someone. Today, Origin Coffee Cambodia ships globally — and the experience from click to cup takes less than two weeks for most international destinations.

Here's everything you need to know to order Cambodia coffee beans online.

## What's Available in OCC's Online Store

OCC's direct-to-consumer store carries:

- **Mondulkiri Fine Robusta** — 100g / 250g / 500g — Natural, Honey, Washed
- **Ratanakiri Microlot** — 100g / 250g — Natural (seasonal)
- **Multi-lot tasting set** — 3×100g — Natural + Honey + Washed
- **Gift box** — 250g or 500g — Choice of processing

All beans are roasted to order — OCC does not hold pre-roasted stock. This means beans are fresh-roasted within 48–72 hours of your order being placed.

## How to Choose Which Beans to Order

**First time ordering?** Start with **Honey Process** — OCC's highest-scoring lot (82–84 SCA) and most universally approachable flavor profile. Brown sugar, stone fruit, creamy body.

**If you love complex, bold coffee:** Go **Natural Process**. Dark chocolate, dried fig, wild berry. OCC's most expressive lot.

**If you prefer clean, easy-drinking:** Choose **Washed Process**. Herbal, mild chocolate, structured. The most restrained and consistent.

**If you want to understand the full range:** Order the **3-lot tasting set** — 100g each of all three processing methods. Cup them side by side.

title: Robusta Coffee Beans Cambodia: Why OCC's Highland Beans Redefine the Species
Author: OCC Team
Summary: Discover why Robusta coffee beans from Cambodia are different. Origin Coffee Cambodia's highland Fine Robusta achieves 80–84 SCA scores — far beyond commodity Robusta. Complete guide.
Excerpt: Robusta has a reputation problem in specialty coffee. OCC is fixing it by producing highland Fine Robusta that scores 80-84 SCA — redefining what Robusta can be.
Keywords: Robusta coffee beans Cambodia, Cambodia Robusta coffee, Fine Robusta beans, highland Robusta Cambodia, OCC Robusta quality
Content:
## Robusta Coffee Beans Cambodia: Why OCC's Highland Beans Redefine the Species

Say 'Robusta coffee beans' to a specialty coffee professional and watch their face. The reaction is usually skepticism, sometimes mild disgust. Robusta is the species of instant coffee, supermarket blends, and the bitter bottom of the espresso cup.

That reputation is earned — for commodity Robusta. For **Fine Robusta from Cambodia's highlands**, it's a misunderstanding that Origin Coffee Cambodia is actively correcting.

## What Makes Cambodia Robusta Beans Different

### Species Baseline

All Robusta shares certain characteristics regardless of origin:

- Higher caffeine (2.2–2.7% vs Arabica's 1.2–1.5%)
- More crema precursors (chlorogenic acid derivatives + lipids)
- Higher body in espresso
- Lower acidity than Arabica
- Different aromatic profile — chocolate, earth, wood (at commodity quality), chocolate, brown sugar, fruit (at Fine Robusta quality)

### The Cambodia Difference

What OCC's highland cultivation changes:

**Altitude** takes commodity Robusta's harsh bitterness and softens it. At 600–900m in Mondulkiri, cooler temperatures reduce the rate at which the harsh chlorogenic acids concentrate in the bean.

**Volcanic soil** adds mineral complexity that lowland Robusta cannot access. Potassium, magnesium, iron — these minerals influence the enzymatic pathways that produce aromatic precursors.

**Selective harvesting** is the most immediately impactful factor. Commodity Robusta is strip-harvested. OCC's Bunong farmers hand-select only fully ripe (dark red) cherries, eliminating the immature cherry defects that define commodity Robusta's bad reputation.

**Specialty processing** converts the cherry's natural potential into cup quality. Natural and honey processing add sweetness and complexity. Washed processing reveals the clean, herbal baseline of the terroir.

title: Single Origin Coffee Cambodia: What Makes OCC's Traceability Real
Author: OCC Team
Summary: What does single origin coffee from Cambodia actually mean? Origin Coffee Cambodia's farm-level traceability, lot documentation, and how OCC defines authentic single origin specialty coffee.
Excerpt: Single origin is one of specialty coffee's most abused terms. OCC's traceability goes from farm to export — every lot is documented, specific, and verifiable.
Keywords: single origin coffee Cambodia, traceable coffee Cambodia, Cambodia single origin lot, OCC traceability, farm to cup Cambodia
Content:
## Single Origin Coffee Cambodia: What Makes OCC's Traceability Real

In specialty coffee, 'single origin' is one of the most abused marketing terms. It can mean anything from 'this coffee is from one country' to 'this lot comes from one specific tree-row on one specific farm, harvested on one specific day.' The difference matters enormously for buyers who use single origin claims on their menus and bags.

Origin Coffee Cambodia's single origin claims are documented, specific, and verifiable. Here's what they actually mean.

## OCC's Traceability Levels

### Level 1: Country of Origin
**Cambodia** — documented by Certificate of Origin from the Cambodia Chamber of Commerce. Every export shipment.

### Level 2: Province
**Mondulkiri Province** or **Ratanakiri Province** — specified in lot documentation. These are distinct growing regions with measurably different soil composition, altitude ranges, and flavor profiles.

### Level 3: Processing Station
Each lot is traceable to the specific processing station where cherry was received, processed, and dried. Station ID is recorded in OCC's internal lot traceability system.

### Level 4: Farmer Group
Lots are traceable to the specific Bunong (or Jarai/Tampuan for Ratanakiri) farmer group that harvested the cherry. Group name, approximate farm location, and harvest date recorded.

### Level 5: Processing Method and Date
Natural / Honey / Washed — specified per lot. Drying start date, completion date, and moisture content at completion recorded.

title: Cambodia Coffee Roast Profile: How to Roast OCC Fine Robusta for Maximum Quality
Author: OCC Team
Summary: Complete roast profile guide for Cambodia coffee beans from OCC. Charge temperature, first crack timing, development ratios, and roast curves for natural, honey, and washed Fine Robusta.
Excerpt: Most roasters approach Robusta with their standard Arabica profile and wonder why the results are confusing. OCC's Fine Robusta requires specific profile adjustments.
Keywords: Cambodia coffee roast profile, how to roast Robusta, Fine Robusta roasting, OCC roast guide, Mondulkiri coffee roast curve
Content:
## Cambodia Coffee Roast Profile: How to Roast OCC Fine Robusta for Maximum Quality

Most roasters approach a new Robusta with their standard Arabica profile — and are confused by the results. OCC's Fine Robusta requires specific profile adjustments that reflect the physical differences between the species. Get these right and you unlock the specialty quality that scored 80–84 on the SCA scale.

## Key Physical Differences That Drive Profile Changes

- **Bean density**: Arabica 1.10–1.20 g/cm³ vs OCC Fine Robusta 1.25–1.35 g/cm³ — Higher charge temp needed
- **First crack temp**: Arabica 196–204°C vs OCC Fine Robusta 205–215°C — Don't drop early
- **First crack intensity**: Arabica sharp/clear vs OCC softer/longer — Listen carefully
- **Chlorogenic acid**: Arabica lower vs OCC higher — Lower drop temp; don't over-develop
- **Sugar content**: Arabica higher vs OCC lower — Longer development needed to build sweetness
- **Moisture content (green)**: Similar for both — standard drying rate

## Recommended Roast Profiles by Application

### Profile 1: Single-Origin Espresso (Medium-Light)

**Target:** Preserve fruit character of natural/honey process; showcase specialty quality

- Charge temperature: 210–215°C (8–12°C above typical Arabica charge)
- Drying phase: 4:30–5:30 (slightly longer — dense bean needs more heat transfer time)
- Maillard phase: Aim for smooth, linear ROR decline
- First crack: ~205–212°C (soft — listen carefully)
- Development time: 60–80 seconds
- DTR: 20–24%
- Drop temperature: 208–213°C
- Agtron: 62–68 (medium-light)

**Expected result:** Fruit-forward, sweet, specialty complexity preserved.

title: Cambodia Green Coffee Beans: Sourcing Unroasted OCC Fine Robusta for Your Roastery
Author: OCC Team
Summary: Source green coffee beans from Cambodia for your roastery. OCC Fine Robusta green bean specifications, export packaging, moisture standards, and how to start green bean sourcing from Mondulkiri.
Excerpt: For specialty roasters, the relationship with a coffee starts at the green bean. OCC's green bean quality is the foundation that makes 80–84 SCA cup scores possible.
Keywords: Cambodia green coffee beans, green bean Cambodia, unroasted coffee Cambodia, OCC green coffee, Cambodia coffee import green bean
Content:
## Cambodia Green Coffee Beans: Sourcing Unroasted OCC Fine Robusta for Your Roastery

For specialty roasters, the relationship with a coffee starts at the green bean — before the roaster fires up. Origin Coffee Cambodia's green bean quality is the foundation that makes 80–84 SCA cup scores possible. This guide covers everything a roaster needs to know about sourcing OCC green beans.

## Green Bean Specifications

Every OCC export lot meets the following green bean standards before release:

- **Species**: Coffea canephora (Robusta)
- **Screen size**: 15+ (primary lots), 14+ (microlots)
- **Moisture content**: 10.5–11.5%
- **Water activity**: <0.65 aw
- **Full defects per 300g**: <5 (specialty threshold)
- **Primary defects**: 0 (insect damage, fungal, etc.)
- **Color**: Uniform blue-green (natural/honey), pale green (washed)
- **Odor**: Clean — no mold, no ferment off-odors
- **Density**: Higher than typical Arabica — adjust charge temperature

## Packaging for Green Bean Export

OCC packages green beans for export in:

**Standard GrainPro-lined jute bags**
- 60kg per bag (standard lot)
- GrainPro inner liner hermetically sealed
- Protects against moisture re-absorption and foreign odors during transit
- Suitable for sea freight and air freight

**Vacuum-sealed bags (for microlots and air freight)**
- 10kg or 30kg sizes
- Nitrogen-flushed before sealing
- Extends freshness window for smaller premium lots

title: Origin Coffee Cambodia Brand Story: Why OCC Exists and What It's Building
Author: OCC Team
Summary: The complete Origin Coffee Cambodia brand story — why OCC was founded, what problem it solves for Cambodian farmers, and how the brand is building Cambodia's specialty coffee future.
Excerpt: Before OCC, Cambodia's coffee was anonymous commodity Robusta. OCC was built to change that by connecting Bunong indigenous farmers with specialty coffee markets.
Keywords: Origin Coffee Cambodia brand story, OCC brand history, Cambodia coffee brand, why Origin Coffee Cambodia, OCC founding story
Content:
## Origin Coffee Cambodia Brand Story: Why OCC Exists and What It's Building

### The Problem OCC Was Built to Solve

Before Origin Coffee Cambodia existed, Cambodia's coffee had a structural problem: the beans were grown in volcanic highland soil by indigenous Bunong farmers who had cultivated coffee for generations — and they were selling it as anonymous commodity Robusta to Vietnamese traders at $1.50–2.00/kg.

The quality potential was real. The market connection was missing. OCC was built to bridge that gap.

## What 'Origin' Means in the Name

The brand name is not accidental. 'Origin' does three things simultaneously:

**Geographic Origin:** Every bag of OCC coffee traces to a specific origin — Mondulkiri Province, a specific farmer group, a specific harvest. Not 'Cambodia' as a vague geography, but a specific place with a specific story.

**Cultural Origin:** The Bunong people's agricultural tradition — shade-grown, forest-integrated, hand-harvested — is the cultural origin of OCC's quality. The brand exists because of that tradition, not despite it.

**Coffee's Origin:** In the specialty world, 'origin' signals the opposite of commercial anonymity. A coffee with an identified origin is a coffee with accountability, traceability, and story. That's OCC's core promise.

## The Mission: Establish Cambodia as a Recognized Specialty Origin

Most coffee-producing countries that are now recognized on the global specialty map — Ethiopia, Colombia, Panama, Kenya — went through a process of deliberate brand-building at the origin level. Individual farms and brands created quality, documented it, marketed it internationally, and over time, the origin became a category.

OCC is doing this for Cambodia. The goal is not just to sell bags of coffee — it is to create a world where specialty buyers automatically include 'Cambodian Fine Robusta' in their origin vocabulary.

Content:
## Cambodia Coffee Subscription: How to Get OCC Fine Robusta Delivered Regularly

The problem with discovering a specialty coffee you love is running out of it. You find OCC's honey process Mondulkiri — rich, sweet, better than you expected — and three weeks later the bag is empty and you're back to whatever's on the shelf.

Origin Coffee Cambodia's subscription program solves this by delivering fresh-roasted Fine Robusta to your door on your schedule.

## How OCC's Subscription Works

**Step 1:** Select your preferred product (honey / natural / washed process, or rotating selection)
**Step 2:** Choose your bag size (100g / 250g / 500g)
**Step 3:** Choose your delivery frequency (every 2 / 4 / 6 / 8 weeks)
**Step 4:** OCC roasts fresh to your schedule and ships via DHL/FedEx
**Step 5:** Pause, modify, or cancel anytime via account portal

## Subscription Options

- **Single Origin** — One processing method, your choice — Every 2–8 weeks — 10% savings
- **Explorer Rotation** — Alternating natural → honey → washed — Every 4 weeks — 12% savings
- **Cupping Box** — 3×100g new/different lots each delivery — Every 4–6 weeks — 15% savings
- **Wholesale Sub** — 1–5kg bulk, any processing — Monthly or quarterly — 8–15% by volume

Content:
## How to Request a Sample from Origin Coffee Cambodia: Step-by-Step Guide

For specialty buyers evaluating a new origin, the sample request is the beginning of everything. It's the moment a supplier relationship either starts or doesn't. Origin Coffee Cambodia's sample program is designed to make this first step easy, informative, and low-commitment.

Here's exactly how it works.

## What OCC's Sample Program Includes

Each sample lot is **250g of roasted whole bean** per processing method. Available options:

- Natural Process Mondulkiri (81–83 SCA)
- Honey Process Mondulkiri (82–84 SCA)
- Washed Process Mondulkiri (80–81 SCA)
- Ratanakiri Microlot (80–82 SCA — subject to availability)

**What arrives with each sample:**

- The 250g sample bag, roasted within 48 hours of dispatch
- Q Grader cupping report for the specific lot (score, attribute breakdown, grader credentials)
- Lot traceability sheet (farmer group, harvest date, processing details)
- Roast profile recommendation card
- Brewing parameter guide

**Sample pricing:** $15–25 per 250g lot (varies by processing method and whether microlot)
**Crediting:** Sample cost is fully credited against your first production order.

title: What Is Robusta Coffee? A Complete Guide for Specialty Coffee Buyers
Author: OCC Team
Summary: Complete guide to Robusta coffee — what it is, how it differs from Arabica, what Fine Robusta means, and why Origin Coffee Cambodia's highland Robusta achieves specialty-grade quality.
Excerpt: Robusta has a reputation problem in specialty coffee. But Fine Robusta — Robusta grown at altitude with specialty protocols — is genuinely different.
Keywords: what is Robusta coffee, Robusta coffee explained, Robusta vs Arabica, what is Fine Robusta, Robusta coffee quality
Content:
## What Is Robusta Coffee? A Complete Guide for Specialty Coffee Buyers

When you encounter 'Robusta' on a specialty coffee label — particularly from Origin Coffee Cambodia — you're encountering a species with a complicated reputation and, increasingly, a legitimate specialty story. Here is everything you need to understand about Robusta coffee.

## The Two Major Coffee Species

Commercial coffee production is dominated by two species:

**Coffea arabica (Arabica):** ~60% of world production. Higher altitude. More complex aromatics. Higher sugar content. More disease-susceptible. Lower caffeine.

**Coffea canephora (Robusta):** ~40% of world production. Lower altitude tolerance. More disease-resistant. Higher caffeine. Higher crema potential. Historically: lower quality in commodity production.

## Why Robusta Got a Bad Reputation

The specialty coffee industry's quality revolution of the 1990s and 2000s was built almost entirely on Arabica. As specialty buyers developed increasingly refined palates, commodity Robusta — harsh, rubbery, defect-laden — became shorthand for 'low quality coffee.'

This conflation was unfair but understandable. **Commodity Robusta is genuinely lower quality** than specialty Arabica. The problem is that specialty coffee's rejection of Robusta threw out the possibility of quality Robusta along with commodity Robusta.
